title: Bacteriophage plaque assay for lytic activity

hypothesis.statement: Phage isolate LP-2024 forms clear plaques on E. coli B strain with titer ≥10⁸ PFU/mL
hypothesis.null_hypothesis: Phage isolate LP-2024 does not produce detectable plaques on E. coli B
hypothesis.why_interesting: Characterizing a novel phage isolate from environmental water sample for potential phage therapy application.

materials_provided.name: Phage lysate LP-2024
materials_provided.specification: Crude lysate in SM buffer, ~10⁶ PFU/mL estimated

failure_conditions.condition: No plaques observed at any dilution
failure_conditions.interpretation: Phage may not infect this host strain
Phage may be temperate (lysogenic) rather than lytic

failure_conditions.condition: Bacterial lawn does not form on control plates

custom_protocol.brief_description: Standard double-layer agar method for bacteriophage enumeration. Phage is mixed with host bacteria in soft agar overlay, poured onto LB agar base, and incubated overnight. Plaques are counted to determine titer.

steps.action: Prepare overnight culture of E. coli B in LB broth at 37°C with shaking
Prepare 10-fold serial dilutions of phage lysate in SM buffer (10⁻¹ to 10⁻⁸)
critical_parameters: Use fresh pipette tips for each dilution

steps.action: For each dilution: Mix 100μL phage dilution + 100μL overnight culture in 3mL molten soft agar (0.7% agar, 48°C). Pour immediately onto pre-warmed LB agar plate.
critical_parameters: Soft agar must be 45-48°C - too hot kills bacteria, too cold solidifies prematurely
Pour quickly and tilt to spread evenly

steps.action: Allow overlays to solidify at room temperature for 15 minutes
steps.expected_outcome: Firm overlay that doesn't slide when tilted
Confluent bacterial lawn with visible plaques

steps.action: Count plaques on plates with 30-300 plaques. Calculate titer as: PFU/mL = (plaques counted) × (dilution factor) × 10
critical_parameters: Count only plates with 30-300 plaques for accuracy
Note plaque morphology: clear, turbid, or halo
steps.expected_outcome: Titer calculation from at least 2 countable dilutions

steps.action: Photograph representative plates showing plaque morphology with ruler for scale
steps.expected_outcome: Clear images of plaques for documentation

safety_notes: E. coli B is BSL-1 but standard microbiological practices should be followed
Dispose of all plates and cultures in biohazard waste
